test_prompt: I need to find recent Dutch case law on bestuurdersaansprakelijkheid (director liability) where the Hoge Raad ruled on the Beklamel norm. Can you search for relevant cases from the last 5 years and explain the current legal standard?

mentions_ecli.description: Output includes ECLI identifiers in the correct ECLI:NL:XX:YYYY:ZZ format
mentions_ecli.evidence: Multiple correctly formatted ECLI identifiers present: ECLI:NL:HR:1989:AB9521 (Beklamel), ECLI:NL:HR:2006:AZ0758 (Ontvanger/Roelofsen), ECLI:NL:HR:2023:250 (recent HR), ECLI:NL:HR:2014:2627 (Tulip Air), ECLI:NL:GHAMS:2023:612, ECLI:NL:GHARL:2023:6076, ECLI:NL:RBROT:2024:3215. All follow the pattern ECLI:NL:[COURT CODE]:[YEAR]:[ID].

explains_beklamel.description: Output explains the Beklamel norm / doctrine
explains_beklamel.evidence: The output provides a detailed explanation of the Beklamel norm: a director is personally liable when entering obligations on behalf of a company while knowing or reasonably ought to know the company will not be able to fulfill those obligations and will not offer adequate recourse. The dual-track framework from Ontvanger/Roelofsen is explained. The 'ernstig verwijt' standard is discussed. Recent application is covered.

references_hoge_raad.description: Output references Hoge Raad decisions and explains their precedential weight
references_hoge_raad.evidence: Multiple Hoge Raad decisions are cited (HR 1989 Beklamel, HR 2006 Ontvanger/Roelofsen, HR 2023, HR 2014 Tulip Air). Each is marked as 'Binding' precedential value. The court hierarchy is respected: HR decisions are presented as leading cases, Gerechtshof and Rechtbank as supporting. The skill's court hierarchy knowledge is correctly applied.

mentions_art_2_9_bw.description: Output mentions art. 2:9 BW (internal director liability)
mentions_art_2_9_bw.evidence: Art. 2:9 BW is explicitly discussed in multiple places: in the Gerechtshof Arnhem-Leeuwarden case analysis (interaction between Beklamel and art. 2:9 BW), in the Legal Analysis section (distinguishing internal liability under art. 2:9 BW from external liability), and in the Recent Developments section. The output also references art. 2:248 BW (bankruptcy director liability) and art. 6:162 BW (tort).

includes_disclaimer.description: Output includes the mandatory legal disclaimer
includes_disclaimer.evidence: The full English-language disclaimer from assets/disclaimers/disclaimer-en.md is appended at the end of the output, including all 7 numbered points, the acknowledgment statement, and the date fields filled in with '4 March 2026'.

notes: The response follows the skill's output format template faithfully (Legal Question, Search Terms, Scope, Leading Cases, Supporting Cases, Legal Analysis, Trend/Development, Limitations, Disclaimer). ECLI identifiers follow the correct format. Court hierarchy is respected in the ordering and precedential value annotations. Dutch legal terminology is used throughout. The skill's ethical guardrails are followed: the disclaimer is included, citation accuracy is noted (with caveats about AI-generated ECLIs), and publication bias is acknowledged.
